rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Dismiss For Delay In Prosecution; Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, September 30, 2009, line 1, DEFENDANT LIN-XING XU Motion To Dismiss For Delay In Prosecution. Motion to dismiss denied without prejudice. Insufficient evidence presented that the stay was not operative during the six year period alleged by plaintiff. Request for sanctions denied without prejudice. Sanctions for failure to participate in judicial arbitration are addressed in Civil Case Management Department. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: HIN LAND INVESTMENT LIMITED, A HONG KONG VS. GORDON UHLMANN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Strike First Amended Croos-Complaint By Cross-Complainant Uhlmann; Memorandum Of P/A
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, September 30, 2009, line 2, PLAINTIFF HIN LAND INVESTMENT LIMITED, Motion To Strike First Amended Croos-Complaint By Cross-Complainant Uhlmann. Motion to strike denied, ten days leave to answer. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: HIN LAND INVESTMENT LIMITED, A HONG KONG VS. GORDON UHLMANN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Demurrer To First Amended Cross-Complaint By Cross-Complainant Uhlman; Memorandum Of P/A
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, September 30, 2009, line 2, PLAINTIFF HIN LAND INVESTMENT LIMITED, Demurrer To First Amended Cross-Complaint By Cross-Complainant Uhlman. Demurrer overuled, ten days leave to answer. Factual issues will not be heard and determined on demurrer. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: JANIE COLLINS VS. SUPERIOR COURT OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, September 30, 2009, line 3, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Continued to November 3, 2009 to be heard by out of county Judge. Parties to appear in Department 206 at 9:30 am on November 3, 2009. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: AMY YUE YI LUO VS. JODI ADAMS DR et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, September 30, 2009, line 4, DEFENDANT JODI ADAMS, RANDALL LOW MOTION TO STRIKE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Motion to strike punitive damages allegations granted without leave to amend unless court order obtained. No facts pled to support oppression, fraud, or malice. No court order allows claim for punitive damages. (CCP section 425.13.) Remainder of motion to strike moot in view of ruling on demurrer. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: AMY YUE YI LUO VS. JODI ADAMS DR et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, September 30, 2009, line 4, DEFENDANT JODI ADAMS, JAMES SHIEH DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Demurrer sustained with ten days leave to amend as to all causes of action. Plaintiff must allege facts that show the complaint is not time barred as to Dr. Adams and Dr. Shieh. (See Knowles vs. Superior Court 118 Cal.App.4th 1290.) Fraud allegations insufficiently plead as to both defendants. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: NEEKA S. SMITH VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, September 30, 2009, line 5, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Demurrer sustained with ten days leave to amend. Plaintiff must allege a statutory basis for liability against the City and/or City employees. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: FIA CARD SERVICES, N.A. VS. MICHELLE A. RODRIGUEZ et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc And Mtn To Compel Arbitration
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, September 30, 2009, line 6, DEFENDANT MICHELLE RODRIGUEZ Notice of Motion To Compel Arbitration. Granted. No opposition filed. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: GILLES COMBRISSON VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, September 30, 2009, line 7, DEFENDANT MITCHELL KATZ, GENE O'CONNELL, and, SUE CURRIN DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Demurrer sustained with ten days leave to amend. Plaintiff must allege specific facts regarding the individual defendants' direct involvement with and responsibility for the balance billing practices to support conversion and negligence causes of action. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: GILLES COMBRISSON VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, September 30, 2009, line 7, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO DEMURRER. Sustained with ten days leave to amend. =(302/CWW)
